changes: Added NETWORK_ERROR_PATTERNS constant with 17 error pattern strings
Added is_network_error helper function for case-insensitive pattern detection
Added 4 new test cases (tests 6-9) for network error detection
file_insights.location: Top of file (constants/functions), bottom of file (test suite)
file_insights.integration_level: Non-breaking addition to existing test suite

patterns_discovered.benefit: Immutable, reusable pattern source of truth
Single point of change, testable isolation
Robust detection across error message variations

patterns_discovered.pattern: Test coverage with positive and negative cases
patterns_discovered.evidence: Tests check detection, non-detection, and case sensitivity
patterns_discovered.benefit: Comprehensive validation of all code paths

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Network error patterns may produce false positives
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Patterns chosen are common error message prefixes; could refine with regex if needed

gotchas_discovered.risk: New network error types from Codex CLI could be missed
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: List is extensible; follow-up monitoring recommended

approach_outcome.execution: Clean implementation in two successful attempts
approach_outcome.test_coverage: 4 new assertions validate core scenarios (connection refused, DNS, success path, case insensitivity)
approach_outcome.code_quality: Follows existing Nushell conventions; integrates seamlessly with test harness

recommendations.suggestion: Consider regex-based pattern matching for more precision
recommendations.rationale: Current substring matching could catch partial matches in unrelated error contexts

recommendations.suggestion: Document why each pattern was selected
recommendations.rationale: Aids future maintainers in understanding error classification scope

recommendations.suggestion: Add integration test with actual Codex CLI failures
recommendations.rationale: Verify pattern detection against real-world error messages from OpenAI

recommendations.suggestion: Ensure is_network_error is called in retry/fallback logic
recommendations.rationale: Helper function only adds value when used to distinguish network errors from other failures
